title: ABC谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot confronts Franklin Clarke and reveals that Franklin is the true "ABC" killer. Poirot explains that the series of murders was a calculated distraction designed to mask a single, premeditated crime. By creating a pattern of murders linked by alphabetical initials, the killer intended to divert attention away from the true motive: the inheritance of Sir Clement Clarke’s estate. While Sir Clement's wife holds life tenure, Franklin stands to inherit the property upon her death.

Poirot identifies Franklin as the mastermind who manipulated Mr. Castor, a timid and indecisive man, to serve as a scapegoat. Franklin used Castor's initials and personality to frame him, even providing Castor with a typewriter and instructions to deliver the letters. The motive for the murders was driven by Franklin's fear of the future; he believed that if Sir Clement married Miss Tora Grey, they might have children, which would permanently deprive Franklin of his inheritance.

To execute the plan, Franklin selected victims whose names and locations matched the ABC pattern. He first killed Mrs. Ascher in Andover. For the "B" victim, he scouted cafes to find Betty Barnard, a girl he courted under the pretense of being married. Poirot concludes that Franklin orchestrated the entire scheme, using Castor as a tool to implement a series of crimes that would provide cover for his ultimate goal of securing his brother's wealth.
